coverage_note: The bundled FAM170A population database is literature oriented and does not include a complete allele-frequency panel. Because ClinGen has not published FAM170A clinical curations, population interpretation should prioritize current ClinVar/gnomAD status, predicted consequence, tissue context, semen phenotype, and independent confirmation.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from FAM170A/ZNFD testis expression, transcription-factor, and male-fertility model literature.

sources.label: NCBI Gene 340069: FAM170A expression and GO context
ClinGen Gene Facts HGNC:27963: clinical curation status
Human Protein Atlas: FAM170A testis protein/RNA context
PubMed 20162441: human ZNFD/FAM170A transcriptional activation study

gene_population_patterns.summary: NCBI and Human Protein Atlas resources frame FAM170A as testis-enriched or testis-relevant, matching the sperm and spermiogenesis literature focus.
Mouse Fam170a deficiency supports a male-fertility and sperm chromatin-remodeling research thesis, but it should not be treated as a validated human diagnostic gene-disease rule.
Human and mouse ZNFD/FAM170A studies support nuclear zinc-finger transcription-factor behavior, including AP1(PMA) and heat-shock-element reporter activity.
Bundled EPIC probes provide local promoter and gene-body methylation context, but no population methylation threshold is bundled for fertility or transcriptional activity.
